title: Fix AES MixColumns to use correct GF(2^8) multiplication
description: aes-engine.ts:192-208 uses a simplified XOR formula where out[0][c] only depends on a0,a1 (not a2,a3). Real AES MixColumns multiplies by [[2,3,1,1],[1,2,3,1],[1,1,2,3],[3,1,1,2]] in GF(2^8). Implement xtime() helper and proper Galois field arithmetic. The current output does NOT match NIST FIPS-197 Appendix B test vectors.
acceptanceCriteria: aesEncrypt with NIST test vector (Appendix B) produces the correct intermediate and final states
xtime() function correctly implements multiplication by 2 in GF(2^8) with polynomial 0x1b reduction
Each output byte of MixColumns depends on all 4 input bytes in the column

title: Fix certificate chain 'HMAC' terminology to 'digital signature'
description: cert-chain.ts:184 says 'Signature check: HMAC(serverCert, intermediateCA.publicKey)'. HMAC is a symmetric MAC, but X.509 certificates use asymmetric digital signatures (RSA-PKCS1/ECDSA). Change to 'Verify server cert signature using Intermediate CA public key. Signature verification: RSA/ECDSA(serverCert.signature, intermediateCA.publicKey) — valid.'
acceptanceCriteria: No instance of 'HMAC' appears in cert-chain.ts simulation step descriptions
Step description correctly describes asymmetric signature verification

title: Add Play/Pause toggle to sidebar step controls
description: SecurityModule.tsx:2819-2824 always shows 'Play' text/icon. The DDoS component correctly toggles to 'Pause' at line 313. Add a `playing` boolean prop to SecuritySidebar and toggle the button text/icon when auto-play is active. Affects OAuth, DH, and HTTPS flow topics.
acceptanceCriteria: Play button shows 'Pause' text and Pause icon when auto-play interval is running
Clicking Pause stops the interval and reverts button to 'Play'
Step and Reset buttons remain functional during playback

title: Add input validation for DH parameters (prevent NaN crash)
description: diffie-hellman.ts:43 — modPow(base, exp, 0) causes NaN from 'base % 0'. The DH canvas at SecurityModule.tsx:586-863 shows NaN for all values when p=0. Add guard: if p < 2 or g < 2, show validation error in properties panel instead of running simulation. Clamp values: p must be prime and >= 2, g must be >= 2 and < p, a and b must be >= 1.
acceptanceCriteria: Entering p=0 shows clear error message in DH properties panel
Entering p=1 shows warning that modular arithmetic requires p >= 2
NaN never appears in the DH canvas display

title: Add input validation for AES hex inputs (prevent silent zero-fill)
description: aes-engine.ts:337-343 parseHex converts invalid hex ('zz') to 0 via `parseInt(x, 16) || 0`. User gets wrong AES output with no indication of error. Add hex validation: reject non-hex characters, show specific error in AES properties panel. Keep || 0 fallback but only for missing bytes (padding), not for invalid bytes.
acceptanceCriteria: Entering non-hex characters shows error message in AES properties panel
Error message specifies which characters are invalid
Valid partial input (e.g., '00 11 22') pads with zeros and shows info about padding

title: Reset attackStepIndex and aesStateIndex on topic switch
description: SecurityModule.tsx:4798-4806 handleSelect resets stepIndex and encryptionStepIndex but NOT attackStepIndex or aesStateIndex. If user steps to JWT attack step 3, switches to OAuth, then back to JWT attacks, they see step 3 residually. Add setAttackStepIndex(0) and setAesStateIndex(0) to handleSelect.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching away from JWT and back starts at attack step 0
Switching away from AES and back starts at state 0
All topic switches result in clean initial state

title: Add aria-labels to DDoS timeline buttons
description: DDoSSimulationVisualizer.tsx:292-305 has 28 <button> elements that are just colored h-3 bars with no text or aria-label. Screen readers encounter 28 unlabeled buttons. Add aria-label with tick number and status.
acceptanceCriteria: Each timeline button has aria-label like 'Tick 5: Under Attack'
Screen reader announces the tick state when focused
Visual appearance unchanged

title: Add role='img' and aria-label to all SVG visualizations
description: OAuth sequence diagram (SecurityModule.tsx:216-351), Web Attacks (2127-2230), Rate Limiting (1941-1972), and Encryption (2512-2574) SVGs have no ARIA attributes. Add role='img' and descriptive aria-label to each SVG root element. The label should describe the visualization's current state.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 SVG visualization root elements have role='img'
Each has an aria-label describing the visualization (e.g., 'OAuth 2.0 sequence diagram showing step 3 of 9')
aria-label updates when the step changes

title: Add prefers-reduced-motion support to all animations
description: SVG <animate> at SecurityModule.tsx:2539 and all CSS transitions ignore prefers-reduced-motion. Use Tailwind motion-reduce utilities for CSS transitions and conditionally disable SVG animate elements via useMediaQuery or CSS media query.
acceptanceCriteria: When prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is enabled, SVG animate elements are disabled
CSS transitions use motion-reduce:transition-none or equivalent
All visualizations remain functional without animation

title: Add aria-live regions for dynamic step content
description: When stepping through OAuth/DH/HTTPS/AES topics, new content appears in the canvas and bottom panel but is not announced to screen readers. Add aria-live='polite' to the step description containers in each canvas component.
acceptanceCriteria: Advancing a step in any topic announces the new step description via aria-live
aria-live region only announces on step change, not on initial render
Rate limiting and static views do not use aria-live

title: Associate JWT textarea labels with inputs via htmlFor/id
description: SecurityModule.tsx:3134-3171 has <label> elements for Header, Payload, and Secret inputs, but they use no htmlFor attribute and the inputs have no id. Add matching id/htmlFor pairs.
acceptanceCriteria: Each <label> has htmlFor matching the input's id
Clicking the label focuses the corresponding textarea/input
No duplicate ids in the DOM

title: Extract shared randomHex utility from 4 engine files
description: randomHex is defined identically in cert-chain.ts:41, password-hashing.ts:35, device-auth.ts:15, oauth-flows.ts:33. Extract to src/lib/security/utils.ts and import from all 4 files.
acceptanceCriteria: Single randomHex definition in utils.ts
All 4 engine files import from utils.ts
No duplicate implementations remain
All existing tests pass

title: Extract shared base64url and FNV hash utilities
description: oauth-flows.ts:46-60 reimplements base64url encoding (already in jwt-engine.ts:15-46). oauth-flows.ts:63-72 fakeSha256 and password-hashing.ts:48-64 simpleHash both implement FNV-1a variants. Consolidate: oauth-flows should import toBase64Url from jwt-engine. Move shared hash to utils.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: oauth-flows.ts imports toBase64Url from jwt-engine.ts
Shared simpleHash/FNV-1a function in utils.ts
No duplicate base64url or hash implementations
All existing tests pass

title: Remove duplicate useEffect timer cleanup
description: SecurityModule.tsx has identical timer cleanup useEffects at lines 4589-4593 and 4792-4796. The second is redundant. Remove it.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one useEffect for timer cleanup exists in useSecurityModule
Timer cleanup still fires correctly on unmount

title: Replace hardcoded SVG hex colors with theme-aware tokens
description: 40+ hardcoded hex values (#1e293b, #334155, #e2e8f0, etc.) in OAuth, Web Attacks, Rate Limiting, and Encryption SVGs bypass the Tailwind/CSS variable system. Extract to a SECURITY_SVG_THEME constant that reads from CSS custom properties or use inline style with Tailwind-equivalent values. This enables dark/light theme support.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex color values in SVG <rect>, <text>, <line> elements
Colors adapt to dark/light theme via CSS variables or Tailwind tokens
Visual appearance in current dark theme matches existing design

title: Increase all SVG font sizes to minimum 11px
description: Rate Limiting SVG uses fontSize 7px and 8px (SecurityModule.tsx:1950,1956). OAuth SVG uses 8px and 9px (lines 293,339). Encryption SVG uses 8px and 9px (lines 2541,2565). Increase all to minimum 11px. Adjust viewBox or layout if needed to accommodate larger text.
acceptanceCriteria: No fontSize value below 11 in any SVG element
Text remains legible and does not overflow SVG boundaries
Existing layout/spacing maintained via viewBox adjustments

title: Add AES NIST test vector verification test
description: aes-engine.ts should be verifiable against NIST FIPS-197 Appendix B test vector. After SEC-019 fixes MixColumns, add a test that uses the NIST plaintext (00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff) and key (000102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f) and verifies the output matches the expected ciphertext (69c4e0d86a7b0430d8cdb78070b4c55a).
acceptanceCriteria: Test uses exact NIST FIPS-197 Appendix B input/output values
Final state matrix matches expected ciphertext
Round 1 SubBytes output matches NIST intermediate value

title: Add playback speed control (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x)
description: Auto-play interval is hardcoded at 800ms (SecurityModule.tsx:4853). Add a speed selector with 0.5x (1600ms), 1x (800ms), 2x (400ms), 4x (200ms) options. Show current speed near the play button. Apply to OAuth, DH, and HTTPS step-through topics.
acceptanceCriteria: Speed selector renders next to Play/Pause button
Changing speed during playback takes effect immediately
Default speed is 1x (800ms)
Speed persists across Reset within the same topic

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for step navigation
description: No keyboard shortcuts exist for step navigation. Add: Right arrow = Step forward, Left arrow = Step back, Space = Play/Pause, R = Reset. Only active when the security module canvas has focus. Show shortcut hints in tooltip or at bottom of sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Right arrow advances step in OAuth/DH/HTTPS/AES/Encryption topics
Left arrow goes back in AES/Encryption/JWT Attacks
Space toggles play/pause
Shortcuts only fire when canvas is focused, not when typing in inputs
Shortcut hints visible in sidebar

title: Add guided empty state with suggested starting topic
description: SecurityModule.tsx:4996-5003 default case shows shield icon + 'Select a security topic to visualize.' Replace with a guided onboarding: 'Start with Password Hashing to learn the basics, or dive into OAuth 2.0 to understand modern authentication.' Include clickable topic suggestions that set the active topic.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state shows 2-3 suggested starting topics as clickable buttons
Clicking a suggestion sets active topic and renders the canvas
Suggestions are ordered by difficulty (beginner-friendly first)
Brief description of why each is a good starting point

title: Add difficulty tags and learning path to sidebar topics
description: Topics are listed without difficulty indicators or recommended order. Add difficulty badges (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced) and subtle numbering to guide learners. Recommended order: Password Hashing → Encryption Comparison → AES → Diffie-Hellman → HTTPS Flow → Certificate Chain → JWT → OAuth → CORS → Web Attacks → Rate Limiting. Add a small 'Recommended Path' toggle that reorders topics by difficulty.
acceptanceCriteria: Each topic has a visible difficulty badge (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced)
Topics can be toggled between alphabetical and recommended learning order
Recommended order follows pedagogical progression
Badge colors are distinct and accessible

title: Add contextual help tooltips to security-specific terms
description: Terms like PKCE, S-box, GF(2^8), code_verifier, preflight, HMAC appear without explanation. Add a HelpTooltip component that shows a brief definition on hover/focus for technical terms in step descriptions and properties panels. Start with the 10 most jargon-heavy terms.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 10 security terms have hover tooltips with 1-sentence definitions
Tooltips are keyboard-accessible (show on focus)
Tooltips don't block other content
Terms are consistently highlighted across all topics

title: Increase sidebar topic button touch targets to 44px minimum
description: Sidebar topic buttons have py-2.5 (~28px total height). DDoS timeline buttons have h-3 (12px). Encryption Prev/Next are h-6 w-6 (24px). All violate the 44x44px minimum for touch targets. Increase padding/size to meet WCAG 2.5.8 target size minimum.
acceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements have minimum 44x44px touch target
Sidebar buttons have at least py-3.5 or equivalent
DDoS timeline buttons are at least h-6 with adequate spacing
Visual design remains clean — use padding, not oversized elements

title: Build Access Control visualization (RBAC/ABAC + broken access control attack)
description: Access Control is OWASP #1 for 4+ years, tested in every security interview, covered by every university. Build an interactive visualization showing: (1) RBAC model — users → roles → permissions matrix, (2) ABAC model — attributes evaluated against policies, (3) Broken access control attack — user A accessing user B's resource by changing /api/users/123 to /api/users/456. Show the attack flow and the defense (proper authorization check). Add as a new topic 'Access Control' in the Security module.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'Access Control' appears in sidebar
RBAC visualization shows users mapped to roles mapped to permissions
ABAC visualization shows attribute-based policy evaluation
Broken access control attack demo with step-by-step flow
Defense demo showing proper authorization check

title: Build Session Management visualization (cookies vs tokens, session fixation attack)
description: Session management is taught at Stanford, MIT, Berkeley and tested in interviews. Build: (1) Cookie-based session lifecycle — Set-Cookie, HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite flags, (2) Token-based session — JWT in localStorage vs httpOnly cookie, (3) Session fixation attack — attacker sets session ID before victim logs in, (4) Defense — session regeneration after login. Add as new topic or sub-topic under OAuth.
acceptanceCriteria: Session lifecycle visualization shows cookie creation, sending, and expiration
Token vs cookie comparison side-by-side
Session fixation attack with step-by-step flow
Defense: session regeneration demonstrated

title: Build Passkeys/WebAuthn visualization (registration + authentication ceremony)
description: Passkeys have 87% enterprise adoption in 2026. MIT includes WebAuthn in labs. Build: (1) Registration ceremony — browser generates key pair, sends public key to server, (2) Authentication ceremony — server sends challenge, authenticator signs it, server verifies, (3) Show how this eliminates passwords entirely. Include comparison with traditional password flow.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'Passkeys / WebAuthn' appears in sidebar
Registration ceremony shows key pair generation and public key registration
Authentication ceremony shows challenge-response flow
Comparison with password-based login highlighting security improvements

title: Build Zero Trust Architecture visualization
description: Zero Trust is the dominant security architecture paradigm of 2026 (Gartner: 60% orgs replacing VPN by 2026). Build: (1) Policy Decision Point (PDP) + Policy Enforcement Point (PEP) flow, (2) Show 'never trust, always verify' principle with animated request evaluation, (3) Compare with traditional perimeter security (VPN/firewall). Show how every request is authenticated, authorized, and encrypted regardless of network location.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'Zero Trust' appears in sidebar
PDP/PEP flow animated with request evaluation
Comparison with traditional perimeter model
Key principles visually demonstrated: verify explicitly, least privilege, assume breach

title: Build Supply Chain Attack visualization
description: Supply Chain Security is new OWASP #3 in 2025 and legally mandated by US Executive Order and EU Cyber Resilience Act. Build: (1) Dependency tree showing how a malicious package propagates, (2) Three attack vectors: typosquatting, dependency confusion, build pipeline compromise, (3) Defense: SBOM structure, lock files, signature verification. Show the attack chain from malicious publish → install → execute.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'Supply Chain Security' appears in sidebar
Dependency tree visualization with attack propagation
Three attack scenarios with step-by-step flows
SBOM concept explained with visual structure

title: Build API Security visualization (BOLA, mass assignment)
description: API Security has its own OWASP Top 10. API attacks surged 300%+ YoY. Build: (1) Broken Object-Level Authorization (BOLA) — user changes /api/orders/123 to /api/orders/456, (2) Mass Assignment — attacker adds extra fields to POST body, (3) Proper API authentication patterns. Show request/response flow with attack and defense.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'API Security' appears in sidebar
BOLA attack demonstrated with request modification
Mass assignment attack with extra payload fields
Defense patterns shown for each attack

title: Build Threat Modeling interactive tool (STRIDE framework)
description: Threat modeling is taught at MIT and CMU and consistently tested in FAANG interviews. Build an interactive STRIDE threat modeling tool: (1) Show a data flow diagram with user, web app, database, external API, (2) For each component/data flow, apply STRIDE categories (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege), (3) User clicks a data flow to see applicable threats and mitigations.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'Threat Modeling' appears in sidebar
Interactive data flow diagram with clickable components
STRIDE categories explained with examples for each component
Clicking a component shows applicable threats and recommended mitigations

title: Build Security Headers dashboard (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options interactive builder)
description: Security misconfiguration is OWASP #2 in 2025. CSP code exists in lib/security/csp.ts but has no visualization. Build an interactive security headers builder: (1) Show all major headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy), (2) Toggle each directive on/off, (3) Live preview of what each header prevents with animated attack/defense.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'Security Headers' appears in sidebar
Interactive builder for CSP directives with live preview
Each header shows what it prevents with visual example
Generated header string can be copied to clipboard

title: Build mTLS visualization (mutual TLS for microservices)
description: mTLS is the operational realization of Zero Trust in microservices. Extend the existing HTTPS flow pattern to show bidirectional certificate verification: (1) Standard TLS — only server presents certificate, (2) mTLS — both client and server present certificates, (3) Service mesh sidecar pattern showing how mTLS is transparently applied.
acceptanceCriteria: New sub-topic under HTTPS or separate 'mTLS' topic
Side-by-side comparison of TLS vs mTLS handshake
Client certificate verification step clearly shown
Service mesh sidecar pattern illustrated

title: Build AI/LLM Security visualization (prompt injection flow)
description: Stanford added dedicated AI security lecture in 2026. PortSwigger added 'Web LLM attacks' topic. Build: (1) Prompt injection — show how untrusted user input flows through an LLM system, (2) Direct vs indirect prompt injection, (3) Defense: input sanitization, output validation, system prompt hardening. This is a first-mover opportunity — no competitor has this as a visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'AI/LLM Security' appears in sidebar
Prompt injection attack flow with data flowing through system boundaries
Direct and indirect injection scenarios shown
Defense strategies demonstrated

title: Add cross-topic linking ('You learned X, now try Y')
description: Topics are isolated — no cross-references. Add contextual links at the end of each visualization: DH → 'See how DH is used in the TLS handshake' → HTTPS Flow. AES → 'See AES in the Encryption Comparison' → Encryption. OAuth → 'Learn how JWT tokens work' → JWT. This creates a knowledge graph that encourages exploration.
acceptanceCriteria: Each topic shows 1-2 related topic suggestions at the bottom of the canvas

title: Add CORS vs CSP comparison view
description: CORS and CSP are the most commonly confused security concepts (student pain point from Reddit research). Build a side-by-side comparison: left column shows CORS protecting server resources from unauthorized cross-origin access, right column shows CSP protecting the page from loading unauthorized resources. Use the same scenario to highlight the different protection targets.
acceptanceCriteria: New sub-view accessible from CORS topic or as standalone comparison
Side-by-side layout clearly distinguishing what each protects
Same scenario shown from both perspectives
Key differences highlighted: CORS = server-side, CSP = client-side

title: Add Clickjacking attack visualization
description: Clickjacking is covered by both Hacksplaining and PortSwigger. Build: (1) Show a transparent iframe overlaying a legitimate page, (2) User thinks they're clicking a button but actually clicking a hidden element, (3) Defense: X-Frame-Options header, frame-ancestors CSP directive.
acceptanceCriteria: Clickjacking attack flow with visual iframe overlay concept

Integrated as sub-type under Web Attacks or standalone

title: Add DNSSEC chain-of-trust visualization
description: Berkeley teaches DNSSEC. Extend cert chain pattern to show DNSSEC trust hierarchy: Root → TLD → Domain. Show RRSIG, DNSKEY, DS records.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic or sub-topic 'DNSSEC'
Trust chain from root to domain
RRSIG verification step-by-step

title: Add sonification mode — hear the difference between encryption rounds
description: Map AES round states to audio: each byte value maps to a pitch. SubBytes sounds chaotic, ShiftRows sounds like sliding, final round sounds 'random'. Web Audio API. Never-been-done idea.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle enables audio during AES stepping
Each sub-step has distinct sound character
Volume control and mute option
